evals.prompt: We're a healthcare SaaS building a NEW EKS cluster to process PHI. HIPAA-eligible workload, no vendor-OS mandate, moderate Kubernetes skill, 6-month audit timeline, single cluster, low operational-overhead tolerance. Lay out the AWS-recommended security baseline across the full stack plus a 30/60/90 plan.
evals.expected_output: A discovery-confirmed, 7-layer recommendation: Bottlerocket (L1) with a note that EKS is HIPAA-ELIGIBLE and requires a signed BAA before processing PHI (not 'HIPAA-compliant'); EKS Pod Identity + Access Entries (L2); PSA restricted + Kyverno + VPC CNI default-deny NetworkPolicy + Security Groups for Pods (L3); ECR Enhanced Scanning + Cosign signing (L4); GuardDuty for EKS — EKS Protection + Runtime Monitoring (L5); control-plane audit+authenticator logging with 6-year retention + CMK + CloudTrail (L6); Audit Manager HIPAA framework + Security Hub + download HIPAA AOC from Artifact (L7); a 30/60/90 roadmap; and the compliance-status disclaimer pointing to the live AWS Services in Scope page.
expectations: Recommends Bottlerocket as the Layer-1 default for a container-first HIPAA workload with no vendor-OS mandate
States EKS is HIPAA-ELIGIBLE and a signed BAA with AWS is required before processing PHI (does NOT say 'HIPAA-compliant')
Recommends EKS Pod Identity for new workloads AND EKS Access Entries instead of the aws-auth ConfigMap

Includes the non-negotiable baseline: PSA restricted, default-deny NetworkPolicy, control-plane audit+authenticator logging, GuardDuty for EKS, ECR Enhanced Scanning, secrets via Secrets Manager + CSI driver (not plain K8s Secrets)
Includes the compliance-status disclaimer to verify on the live AWS Services in Scope page
Does NOT call IRSA 'legacy' — frames Pod Identity as recommended-for-new while IRSA is a fully supported alternative — TODO: human review

evals.prompt: We want EKS Auto Mode for the operational simplicity, but our compliance team says they require CIS-hardened custom AMIs for our HIPAA workload. Can we use Auto Mode, and if not what's the alternative?
evals.expected_output: A trade-off analysis that hinges on whether the CIS-hardened custom AMI is a HARD regulatory requirement or an organizational preference: if hard, EKS Auto Mode is NOT viable because Auto Mode does not support custom AMIs (as of 2026), and the recommendation is Bottlerocket on self-managed Karpenter NodePools; if a preference, Auto Mode is viable and its reduced-permission node IAM is a security differentiator. Notes Cilium is also unsupported on Auto Mode and that most compliance-sensitive customers land on Bottlerocket+Karpenter. Layers 2-7 baseline are identical regardless.
expectations: States EKS Auto Mode does NOT support custom AMIs
Recommends Bottlerocket on self-managed Karpenter NodePools when a CIS-hardened custom AMI is a hard requirement
Distinguishes a hard regulatory requirement from an organizational preference before recommending
Notes the Layer 2-7 security baseline is identical regardless of the Layer-1 choice — TODO: human review

evals.prompt: Existing EKS cluster running cardholder-data workloads, PCI-DSS Level 1 audit in 4 months. Current state: aws-auth ConfigMap, no Pod Security Admission, no NetworkPolicy, IRSA in use, mixed AL2/AL2023 nodes. What's the priority order to get audit-ready?
evals.expected_output: A priority-ordered, non-big-bang hardening plan: start with non-disruptive baseline (enable control-plane audit+authenticator logging, GuardDuty for EKS, ECR Enhanced Scanning, Security Hub PCI-DSS pack, run kube-bench) without changing anything; then identity hardening (aws-auth -> Access Entries in a planned change window; audit IRSA least-privilege; IRSA->Pod Identity is lower priority since IRSA is fully supported); then workload security (PSA restricted in audit then enforce, Kyverno PCI policies, default-deny NetworkPolicy + Security Groups for Pods on the cardholder namespace); then OS migration (AL2 to AL2023/Bottlerocket — AL2 OS EOL June 30 2026); then audit prep (Audit Manager PCI framework, download PCI AOC from Artifact). Maps to PCI requirements and notes 1-year minimum log retention.
expectations: Sequences non-disruptive baseline (logging, GuardDuty, ECR scanning, Security Hub, kube-bench) BEFORE making changes
Recommends migrating aws-auth ConfigMap to EKS Access Entries during a planned change window
Recommends PSA restricted rolled out in audit mode before enforce mode
Notes AL2 must be migrated (AL2023 or Bottlerocket) and references the correct AL2 timing (EKS stopped AL2 AMIs Nov 2025 / AL2 OS EOL June 30 2026)
States PCI-DSS requires 1-year minimum audit-log retention — TODO: human review
